text.title: Outsource PR Etiquette Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: PR body says 'fixes login bug and refactors session storage and adds telemetry'.
antipattern.root-cause: Author batched unrelated work to reduce PR count, not effort.
antipattern.fix: Split into three PRs along the listed intents; each opens against a separate branch.

antipattern.symptom: Reviewer replies 'how do I run this' 18 hours later, blocking landing for another day.
antipattern.root-cause: Author assumed reviewer has the same local setup.
antipattern.fix: Add the Repro section with clone+checkout+run commands and the expected output line.

antipattern.symptom: PR is 1.2k LOC across 28 files with no waiver_reason set.
antipattern.root-cause: Methodology rule size-cap-400 ignored; reviewer fatigues and rubber-stamps.
antipattern.fix: Either split the PR by intent OR set waiver_reason and call out the unavoidable scope.

antipattern.symptom: Production incident; on-call cannot figure out how to back out the change quickly.
antipattern.root-cause: Rollback plan was not captured at PR time.
antipattern.fix: Block merge until the body has a one-sentence rollback (revert SHA, feature flag, or DB step).
